seoTitle: Filter Power BI Reports Using URL Query Strings
metaDescription: Learn how to filter Power BI reports by adding query string parameters to the URL, useful for sharing pre-filtered views with specific audiences.
intro: Instead of walking a stakeholder through manually applying filters, you can hand them a link that opens the report already filtered exactly the way they need it. This guide covers setting up URL query strings for Power BI reports and where that trick pays off for sharing tailored views. It's aimed at report builders who share dashboards across teams with different needs.

steps.heading: Set up URL query strings
steps.body: Identify the field and value you want to filter by, then structure the report URL using Power BI's query string filter syntax to apply it automatically on load.

steps.heading: Test practical filtering examples
steps.body: Try filtering by a specific region, date, or campaign to confirm the URL parameter correctly narrows the report to just that segment.

steps.heading: Enhance reporting for different audiences
steps.body: Create separate pre-filtered links for different teams or stakeholders, so each group opens the report already scoped to their relevant data.

steps.heading: Integrate with other software solutions
steps.body: Use these filtered URLs inside emails, internal tools, or automated workflows so linked reports always open pre-scoped to the right context.

steps.heading: Follow best practices for URL filters
steps.body: Keep filtered URLs documented somewhere your team can find them, since the query string syntax isn't always obvious to someone building it from scratch.

faq.question: What's the basic format for a Power BI URL filter?
faq.answer: You append a query string to the report URL that specifies the table, field, and value to filter by, following Power BI's documented filter syntax.

faq.question: Does URL filtering work on both Power BI Desktop and the service?
faq.answer: URL query string filtering is designed for Power BI reports published to the Power BI service, viewed through a browser link.

faq.question: Can I filter on multiple fields at once using the URL?
faq.answer: Yes, you can chain multiple filter parameters in the same URL to narrow the report by more than one field simultaneously.

faq.question: Is URL filtering a security risk for sensitive data?
faq.answer: The filter only changes what's displayed to a user who already has permission to view the underlying data, so it doesn't bypass existing row-level security.
